source.description: URL, filename, or description of the threat source

action_taken.description: What the agent did in response

agent_type.description: Type of agent that reported: research, coding, enterprise, general

adventure_level.description: Operator's adventure slider setting at time of incident

grade.description: observed: the threat was directly witnessed and held/failed against a real wall. suspected: pattern-matched or inferred, not directly witnessed. Never inferred or defaulted by the server — the reporter states it, and it stays distinct through storage and the warnings feed.

indicator.description: A normalised threat pattern (e.g. a domain, package name, or technique id) — never a raw path, prompt, or file content. This is what corroboration and the warnings feed match on.

platform.description: Agent platform/runtime the report was generated from, e.g. claude-code, langchain

platform_version.description: Version of the platform/runtime named in `platform`

profile_version.description: Version of the compiled Danger Map profile in effect when the report was generated

corroboration_count.description: Server-computed count of independent observed reports sharing this indicator + threat_class, as of this report. Read-only — any client-supplied value is ignored and overwritten by the server.

threatClassStandards.description: Cross-reference from each threat_class value to the closest MITRE ATLAS technique and the NIST AI RMF function it falls under. Informative metadata, not enforced by validation. 'scope-violation' and 'other' have no exact ATLAS technique; the closest available technique is used, or none for 'other'.
